zh.title: 雪崩币/美元技术分析：横盘整理偏多格局
zh.content:
当前AVAX/USD价格围绕6.86窄幅震荡，整体呈弱势横盘略偏多结构，价格稳居20日均线6.81上方，短线多空拉锯明显，趋势尚未形成单边突破，市场进入整理阶段

MACD指标微幅为正0.035，动能偏多但缺乏放量配合，RSI缺失但从结构看未进入超买区域，短期动能略有抬升但持续性仍待验证，整体偏震荡上行

下方关键支撑位于6.81与6.86低点附近，若失守可能回测6.75区域，上方阻力关注6.90及7.00整数关口，当前区间波动收敛，突破需成交量确认

操作上建议以区间低吸高抛为主，激进者可在6.82附近轻仓试多，止损设于6.75下方，若放量突破6.90则顺势加仓，谨防假突破与流动性收缩风险

en.title: Avalanche vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
AVAX/USD is currently in a very tight consolidation phase, trading around 6.8676 with minimal volatility over the last session. Price action is hovering near the upper boundary of a micro-range between 6.8625 and 6.8676, indicating equilibrium between buyers and sellers. The broader structure remains slightly bullish as price holds above the 20-day SMA at 6.81273, suggesting underlying accumulation rather than distribution.

Momentum signals are limited but slightly positive, with MACD showing a small bullish reading at 0.035121896, implying mild upward pressure. However, the absence of a visible signal line and RSI value restricts confirmation of strength, making momentum assessment incomplete. The SMA(20) continues to act as a dynamic support level, reinforcing the idea of a controlled uptrend rather than an impulsive breakout phase environmental.

Immediate support is established near 6.8625, aligning with the session low and recent consolidation floor. A secondary support zone is found around the 20-day SMA at 6.81273, which would be critical if selling pressure increases. On the upside, resistance is concentrated at 6.8676, with a breakout potentially opening room toward 6.90 and the psychological 7.00 level if momentum expands soon.

Trading conditions favor range-bound strategies in the short term, with mean reversion trades near support and profit-taking near resistance. Conservative traders may wait for a confirmed breakout above 6.8676 before entering long positions, targeting extension toward 6.90. Risk management remains essential, with stops below 6.8625 to avoid false breakdowns in this low-volatility environment. Volatility compression suggests that any breakout move could be sharp once liquidity returns.
